# Transcrição: 12-What Is a Coach

- **Fonte:** 12-What Is a Coach
- **Duração:** 01:57
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:57]

What is a coach? What do Bill Gates, Steve Jobs and Oprah Winfrey have in common? They all had coaches. Some people need more accountability, extra attention and more support. That's where you as a coach come in. Based on your area of expertise, you'll work with your clients one-on-one or in a small group setting to help them set goals, develop action plans and hold them accountable to their progress. You'll also help them identify obstacles while teaching them the skills, strategies and tools to break through to success. Plus, you'll support, encourage and celebrate your ideal audience every step of the way. Virtually every industry and niche is open to coaching and no license or certification is required to call yourself a coach. There are coaches for leadership, marketing, sales, social media, organization, communication, mindset as well as for specific skills such as sleep, public speaking, writing, decluttering and more. The coaching path is a fantastic option if you're passionate about working with your ideal audience in a more personalized way. If you're a strong communicator who can clearly articulate ideas in real time, if you're an active listener who hears what people say and what they don't if you have emotional intelligence and can deeply understand your ideal client's fears, motivations and internal obstacles. Coaching might be challenging if you feel uncomfortable in one-on-one situations, if you have difficulty setting boundaries or have limited empathy and patience. However, if you have strong interpersonal skills, enjoy working with people one-on-one or small groups, want to offer more personalized support and attention and can hold people accountable while supporting and empowering them as they grow, then the coaching path might be right for you.
